feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: TOI-4552 b: A new ultra-short period rocky world revealed by NIRPS and TESS
entry.content:
Authors: A Srivastava, R Doyon, F Bouchy, É Artigau, C Cadieux... - arXiv preprint arXiv ..., 2026

Abstract: A particularly intriguing subclass of rocky exoplanets are the ultra-short period (USP) worlds that orbit their host stars in less than a day. These planets are particularly rare around M dwarf stars, with so far only ten that have a constrained mass and radius ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Elsa Ducrot (author alert)

entry.title: The atmosphere of the temperate exo-Saturn TOI-199b with JWST
entry.content:
Authors: A Bello-Arufe, R Hu, M Zilinskas, J Yang, A Tokadjian... - ... Society Meeting Abstracts, 2026

Abstract: Temperate gas giants represent an unexplored frontier in exoplanet atmospheric characterization. Orbiting a G-type star every~ 100 days, the Saturn-mass exoplanet TOI-199 b is one of the most favorable low-temperature gas giants for atmospheric ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Renyu Hu (author alert)

entry.title: 1Scientific Data|(2022) 9: 102| https://doi. org/10.1038/s41597-022-01210-y www. nature. com/scientificdata Open access dataset of task-free hemodynamic activity in ...
entry.content:
Authors: B Blanco, M Molnar, M Carreiras, C Caballero-Gaudes

Abstract: 1Scientific Data | (2022) 9:102 | https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-022-01210-y www.nature.com/scientificdata Open access dataset of task-free hemodynamic activity in 4-month- old infants during sleep using fNIRS Borja Blanco 1,2 ✉, Monika Molnar3, Manuel Carreiras1 & ...

Alert: Manuel Carreiras (author alert)

entry.title: TOI-4552 b: A new ultra-short period rocky world revealed by NIRPS and TESS
entry.content:
Authors: A Srivastava, R Doyon, F Bouchy, É Artigau, C Cadieux... - arXiv preprint arXiv ..., 2026

Abstract: A particularly intriguing subclass of rocky exoplanets are the ultra-short period (USP) worlds that orbit their host stars in less than a day. These planets are particularly rare around M dwarf stars, with so far only ten that have a constrained mass and radius ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Björn Benneke (author alert)

entry.title: Using Machine Learning to Identify Brown Dwarfs in Deep JWST Surveys
entry.content:
Authors: J Haynes, E Pritchard, M Loritsch, E Softich... - ... Society Meeting Abstracts, 2026

Abstract: Abstract The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) has dramatically enhanced our ability to observe faint sources in deep-field surveys, revealing a distant and ancient population of brown dwarfs likely originating from the Milky Way's metal poor thick ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Adam J. Burgasser (author alert)

entry.title: What is quantum biology?
entry.content:
Authors: GD Scholes, GR Fleming - Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, 2026

Abstract: Quantum biology is the field at the intersection of quantum-related physics and the biology of living systems. The goal of the field is to determine if quantum phenomena underpin biological function at the macroscale. Such results, supported by ...

Alert: Graham Fleming (author alert)

entry.title: What is quantum biology?
entry.content:
Authors: GD Scholes, GR Fleming - Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, 2026

Abstract: Quantum biology is the field at the intersection of quantum-related physics and the biology of living systems. The goal of the field is to determine if quantum phenomena underpin biological function at the macroscale. Such results, supported by ...

Alert: Gregory Scholes (author alert)

entry.title: Photoreceptor control of Platynereis growth and lifespan via evolutionarily conserved molecular pathways
entry.content:
Authors: G Andreatta, F Scaramuzza, A Ćorić, L Orel, AM Mat... - Proceedings of the National ..., 2026

Abstract: Natural light is severely affected by human impact on Earth, yet little is known about the roles light receptors have outside vision and rhythmic processes, despite their tremendously wide abundance. Here we show that loss-of-function of the light ...

Alert: Kristin Tessmar-Raible (author alert)
